CASE STUDY · PROJECT 01 · FEATURED

AI Research Agent

A self-hosted multi-tool AI research agent — live streaming, terminal-themed, and actually deployed.

LangGraphGPT-4oFastAPI PineconeOpenAI EmbeddingsSerpAPI Server-Sent EventsPythonJavaScript
// DEMO VIDEO

Can't play inline? Open in Google Drive ↗

The Problem

The original research agent was a Jupyter notebook — a GPT-4o oracle powered by LangGraph that could route between multiple tools to answer complex questions. It worked, but it was locked in a development environment. Nobody else could use it.

The goal: turn that notebook into a real deployed web product — proper streaming UI, admin panel to populate the knowledge base, rate limiting, and production-grade error handling.

How It Works

Built on a LangGraph state machine. An "oracle" node (GPT-4o) decides which tool to invoke, routes there, gets the result, and loops until it has enough to compile a final report.

rag_search
Semantic search across all ingested papers in Pinecone
rag_search_filter
Same search filtered to one specific ArXiv paper
fetch_arxiv
Live abstract lookup from arxiv.org
web_search
Google search results via SerpAPI

In practice: 11,800+ chunks indexed, answers research questions by routing across all four tools in a single query, streamed live to the browser over Server-Sent Events.

Bugs I Actually Fixed
  • Silent form submission failure: Multi-field forms don't submit on Enter per the HTML spec. Fixed by adding explicit run/ingest buttons and switching from EventSource to fetch-based streaming (EventSource can't read error response bodies).
  • ArXiv query syntax: The API requires a field prefix (all:, cat:, au:). Added auto-normalization that wraps bare input in all:.
  • Pinecone vector shape: Passed a list-of-one-vector where Pinecone expected a flat vector — silent bug, wrong results without crashing. Fixed with embed_texts([query])[0].
  • Rate limiting: Added per-IP rolling-window rate limiter with Retry-After headers tested live.

CASE STUDY · PROJECT 02 · KAGGLE CAPSTONE

Career Coach AI Agent

Upload your resume and get a full career analysis powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash — built for Kaggle's 5-Day AI Agents Intensive.

Gemini 2.5 FlashGoogle GenAI SDK StreamlitAgentic AIPDF Parsing PythonConcierge Agent
// DEMO VIDEO

Can't play inline? Open in Google Drive ↗

The Problem

Personalised career guidance is expensive and inaccessible. This project — built as the capstone for Kaggle's 5-Day AI Agents Intensive — delivers a virtual career mentor that analyses your actual resume and produces actionable guidance in real time.

Six-Step Agent Workflow
Resume Upload
PDF parsed and sent to Gemini for extraction
Skill Analysis
Extracts Python, Java, SQL, AI/ML skills from resume
Career Recommendation
Suggests roles like Data Analyst, AI Engineer
Skill Gap Detection
Compares current skills vs target role requirements
Learning Roadmap
Week-by-week structured plan to close the gaps
Interview Prep
Technical, HR, and project-based questions

Why Gemini 2.5 Flash? Fast response times + strong instruction-following + cost-efficient inference = right for a multi-step real-time coaching workflow where every delay breaks the experience.

CASE STUDY · PROJECT 03 · ML / GROUP PROJECT

Handwritten Equation Solver

Draw a maths equation on a canvas — 98.38% accurate SVM classifier trained on 48 stroke-based features recognises each symbol, SymPy solves it live.

SVMscikit-learnFlask SymPyGridSearchCV PythonHTML CanvasCSE3008 ML Course
// DEMO VIDEO

Can't play inline? Open in Google Drive ↗

The Problem

Recognising handwritten mathematical symbols is harder than text — symbols like ÷, (, ) are small, look similar, and vary widely between writers. Standard OCR pipelines don't generalise well to math notation.

This group project (Manogna, Sauryadipta, Partha) for CSE3008 Machine Learning built a full pipeline: draw on canvas → extract stroke features → classify each symbol → solve the equation.

48 Features Per Symbol

Each stroke is first resampled to 30 evenly spaced points (removing speed variation) and normalised to a 1×1 unit square (removing size/position effects). Then 48 features are extracted:

  • Base features (6): number of strokes, width, height, aspect ratio, total stroke length, bounding box area
  • Direction histogram (8): stroke angle histogram — critical for ( vs )
  • Curvature statistics (4): mean, std, max, total curvature — separates curved from angular symbols
  • Endpoints (4): start/end (x,y) of first stroke — encodes stroke order and orientation
  • Direction changes (1): number of significant cusps — useful for symbols with sharp turns
  • Grid zone densities (25): point density in a 5×5 spatial grid — fine-grained local shape information
Model Comparison
ModelTest AccuracyNotes
SVM (RBF kernel)98.38%Chosen — GridSearchCV tuned over C and gamma
Gradient Boosting98.30%Close second, used for benchmarking
Random Forest98.05%Good, slightly lower
KNNLowerStruggled with similar-looking symbols
Logistic RegressionLowerOvR multi-class reduced accuracy

CASE STUDY · PROJECT 04 · ANDROID / GROUP PROJECT

VIT-AP Emergency Alert App

Press the power button 4 times — a GPS-tagged email reaches medical or security staff within 5 seconds. Built for Engineering Clinics, team of 6.

Android SDKJavaFirebase Gmail SMTPFusedLocationProvider AccessibilityServiceSharedPreferences
The Problem

VIT-AP is a large campus. In a medical emergency, finding staff, making a call, or navigating to an office wastes critical time. There was no quick automated way for a student to alert the right department with their exact location.

Group project of 6 (Thota Manogna, Sreepathi Pujitha, Kode Bhuvaneswari, Sripurapu Lavanya, Yendu Thanushka, Sri Lakshmi) under Prof. GDV SanthoshKumar for Engineering Clinics (ECE).

Architecture
MainActivity
Role select — Student or Admin (passcode-gated)
LoginActivity
Student fills name, email, block, room → SharedPreferences
EmergencyActivity
Health / Normal buttons + GPS fetch + email dispatch
GMailSender
JavaMail SMTP over SSL port 465 to gmail.com
SOSAccessibilityService
Counts power button presses → triggers SOS at 4
AdminActivity
Admin updates medical/security email addresses

Tested result: Emails reached the correct department within 5 seconds of trigger. GPS links accurately redirected to live location on Google Maps. Auto-SOS triggered correctly after 4 power button presses on multiple Android devices.

Source Code
GMailSender.java — SMTP email dispatch via JavaMail 
package com.example.vitapemergencyalertapp;

import java.util.Properties;
import javax.mail.Authenticator;
import javax.mail.Message;
import javax.mail.PasswordAuthentication;
import javax.mail.Session;
import javax.mail.Transport;
import javax.mail.internet.InternetAddress;
import javax.mail.internet.MimeMessage;

public class GMailSender {

    private final String user;
    private final String password;

    public GMailSender(String user, String password) {
        this.user = user;
        this.password = password;
    }

    public void sendMail(String subject, String body, String sender, String recipients) throws Exception {
        Properties props = new Properties();
        props.put("mail.smtp.host", "smtp.gmail.com");
        props.put("mail.smtp.port", "465");
        props.put("mail.smtp.socketFactory.port", "465");
        props.put("mail.smtp.socketFactory.class", "javax.net.ssl.SSLSocketFactory");
        props.put("mail.smtp.auth", "true");

        Session session = Session.getInstance(props, new Authenticator() {
            protected PasswordAuthentication getPasswordAuthentication() {
                return new PasswordAuthentication(user, password);
            }
        });

        MimeMessage message = new MimeMessage(session);
        message.setSender(new InternetAddress(sender));
        message.setSubject(subject);
        message.setText(body);
        message.setRecipient(Message.RecipientType.TO, new InternetAddress(recipients));

        Transport.send(message);
    }
}
EmergencyActivity.java — GPS fetch + email send logic 
package com.example.vitapemergencyalertapp;

import android.Manifest;
import android.content.Intent;
import android.content.SharedPreferences;
import android.content.pm.PackageManager;
import android.location.Location;
import android.os.Bundle;
import android.widget.Button;
import android.widget.Toast;

import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog;
import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity;
import androidx.core.app.ActivityCompat;
import androidx.core.content.ContextCompat;

import com.google.android.gms.location.FusedLocationProviderClient;
import com.google.android.gms.location.LocationServices;

public class EmergencyActivity extends AppCompatActivity {

    private FusedLocationProviderClient fusedLocationClient;
    private static final int LOCATION_PERMISSION_REQUEST = 1;

    private static final String SENDER_EMAIL = "manogna.24bce7808@vitapstudent.ac.in";
    private static final String SENDER_PASSWORD = "enlgawlxozwwfhgo"; // Gmail App Password

    private Button btnHealth, btnNormal, btnLogout;

    private String name, email, block, room;

    @Override
    protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState);
        setContentView(R.layout.activity_emergency);

        fusedLocationClient = LocationServices.getFusedLocationProviderClient(this);

        btnHealth = findViewById(R.id.btnHealth);
        btnNormal = findViewById(R.id.btnNormal);
        btnLogout = findViewById(R.id.btnLogout);

        // ✅ Load user data (from intent OR SharedPreferences)
        name = getIntent().getStringExtra("name");
        email = getIntent().getStringExtra("email");
        block = getIntent().getStringExtra("block");
        room = getIntent().getStringExtra("room");

        if (name == null || email == null || block == null || room == null) {
            SharedPreferences prefs = getSharedPreferences("UserPrefs", MODE_PRIVATE);
            name = prefs.getString("name", "Unknown");
            email = prefs.getString("email", "Unknown");
            block = prefs.getString("block", "Unknown");
            room = prefs.getString("room", "Unknown");
        }

        // ✅ Ask permission for location once
        if (ContextCompat.checkSelfPermission(this, Manifest.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION)
                != PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED) {
            ActivityCompat.requestPermissions(
                    this,
                    new String[]{Manifest.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION},
                    LOCATION_PERMISSION_REQUEST
            );
        }

        // ✅ Health Emergency -> confirmation + email alert
        btnHealth.setOnClickListener(v -> showHealthAlertDialog());

        // ✅ Normal Emergency -> confirmation + simple email alert
        btnNormal.setOnClickListener(v -> {
            new AlertDialog.Builder(this)
                    .setTitle("Confirm Normal Emergency")
                    .setMessage("Are you sure you want to send a Normal Emergency alert?")
                    .setPositiveButton("Yes", (dialog, which) -> getLastLocationAndSendNormal())
                    .setNegativeButton("Cancel", null)
                    .show();
        });

        // ✅ Logout -> clear saved info and go to MainActivity
        btnLogout.setOnClickListener(v -> {
            getSharedPreferences("UserPrefs", MODE_PRIVATE).edit().clear().apply();
            Intent i = new Intent(EmergencyActivity.this, MainActivity.class);
            startActivity(i);
            finish();
        });

        // ✅ AUTO SOS TRIGGER (Power button 4x)
        boolean autoHealth = getIntent().getBooleanExtra("autoHealth", false);
        if (autoHealth) {
            Toast.makeText(this, "Auto Health Emergency Triggered!", Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show();
            getLastLocationAndSend();
        }
    }

    private void showHealthAlertDialog() {
        new AlertDialog.Builder(this)
                .setTitle("Confirm Health Emergency")
                .setMessage("Are you sure you want to send a Health Emergency alert?")
                .setPositiveButton("Yes", (dialog, which) -> getLastLocationAndSend())
                .setNegativeButton("Cancel", null)
                .show();
    }

    private void getLastLocationAndSend() {
        if (ActivityCompat.checkSelfPermission(this, Manifest.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION)
                != PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED &&
                ActivityCompat.checkSelfPermission(this, Manifest.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION)
                        != PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED) {
            Toast.makeText(this, "Location permission not granted", Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show();
            return;
        }

        fusedLocationClient.getLastLocation().addOnSuccessListener(this, location -> {
            if (location != null) {
                sendEmergencyEmail(location);
            } else {
                Toast.makeText(this, "Unable to get location", Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show();
            }
        });
    }

    private void getLastLocationAndSendNormal() {
        if (ActivityCompat.checkSelfPermission(this, Manifest.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION)
                != PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED &&
                ActivityCompat.checkSelfPermission(this, Manifest.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION)
                        != PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED) {
            Toast.makeText(this, "Location permission not granted", Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show();
            return;
        }

        fusedLocationClient.getLastLocation().addOnSuccessListener(this, location -> {
            if (location != null) {
                sendNormalEmail(location);
            } else {
                Toast.makeText(this, "Unable to get location", Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show();
            }
        });
    }

    private void sendEmergencyEmail(Location location) {
        new Thread(() -> {
            try {
                String type = "Health Emergency";
                String subject = "🚨 VIT-AP Emergency Alert: " + type;
                String body = "A health emergency has been reported.\n\n" +
                        "Type: " + type + "\n" +
                        "Location: https://www.google.com/maps?q=" +
                        location.getLatitude() + "," + location.getLongitude() + "\n\n" +
                        "Block: " + block + "\n" +
                        "Room: " + room + "\n" +
                        "Reported by: " + name + " (" + email + ")";

                EmailStorage storage = new EmailStorage(EmergencyActivity.this);
                String targetEmail = storage.getMedicalEmail();

                GMailSender sender = new GMailSender(SENDER_EMAIL, SENDER_PASSWORD);
                sender.sendMail(subject, body, SENDER_EMAIL, targetEmail);

                runOnUiThread(() ->
                        Toast.makeText(EmergencyActivity.this, "Health Alert Sent Successfully!", Toast.LENGTH_LONG).show());
            } catch (Exception e) {
                runOnUiThread(() ->
                        Toast.makeText(EmergencyActivity.this, "Error: " + e.getMessage(), Toast.LENGTH_LONG).show());
            }
        }).start();
    }

    private void sendNormalEmail(Location location) {
        new Thread(() -> {
            try {
                String type = "Normal Emergency";
                String subject = "⚠️ VIT-AP Emergency Alert: " + type;
                String body = "A normal emergency has been reported.\n\n" +
                        "Type: " + type + "\n" +
                        "Block: " + block + "\n" +
                        "Room: " + room + "\n" +
                        "Reported by: " + name + " (" + email + ")";

                EmailStorage storage = new EmailStorage(EmergencyActivity.this);
                String targetEmail = storage.getSecurityEmail();

                GMailSender sender = new GMailSender(SENDER_EMAIL, SENDER_PASSWORD);
                sender.sendMail(subject, body, SENDER_EMAIL, targetEmail);

                runOnUiThread(() ->
                        Toast.makeText(EmergencyActivity.this, "Normal Alert Sent Successfully!", Toast.LENGTH_LONG).show());
            } catch (Exception e) {
                runOnUiThread(() ->
                        Toast.makeText(EmergencyActivity.this, "Error: " + e.getMessage(), Toast.LENGTH_LONG).show());
            }
        }).start();
    }
}
SOSAccessibilityService.java — power button SOS detection 
package com.example.vitapemergencyalertapp;

import android.accessibilityservice.AccessibilityService;
import android.content.BroadcastReceiver;
import android.content.Context;
import android.content.Intent;
import android.content.IntentFilter;
import android.os.Handler;
import android.os.Looper;
import android.view.accessibility.AccessibilityEvent;
import android.widget.Toast;

/**
 * Accessibility service that listens for screen ON/OFF events and triggers an SOS
 * when the power button is pressed multiple times quickly.
 *
 * Notes:
 * - You must add the service entry to AndroidManifest.xml and the res/xml config (sos_service_config.xml).
 * - The user must ENABLE this Accessibility service manually in Settings -> Accessibility.
 */
public class SOSAccessibilityService extends AccessibilityService {

    private static final int TIME_WINDOW_MS = 10000; // 10 seconds window
    private static final int PRESS_TARGET = 4;       // number of presses to trigger SOS

    private int pressCount = 0;
    private long firstPressTime = 0;

    private final Handler mainHandler = new Handler(Looper.getMainLooper());
    private ScreenReceiver screenReceiver;

    @Override
    public void onCreate() {
        super.onCreate();
        // nothing here — registration is done in onServiceConnected()
    }

    @Override
    protected void onServiceConnected() {
        // Called when the accessibility service is enabled by the user
        mainHandler.post(() -> Toast.makeText(this, "SOS monitor active", Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show());

        // Register receiver for screen ON/OFF
        screenReceiver = new ScreenReceiver();
        IntentFilter filter = new IntentFilter();
        filter.addAction(Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_OFF);
        filter.addAction(Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_ON);
        registerReceiver(screenReceiver, filter);
    }

    @Override
    public void onAccessibilityEvent(AccessibilityEvent event) {
        // We don't need to handle accessibility events for this feature.
    }

    @Override
    public void onInterrupt() {
        // Required override; nothing to do here.
    }

    @Override
    public void onDestroy() {
        // Unregister receiver to avoid leaks
        try {
            if (screenReceiver != null) {
                unregisterReceiver(screenReceiver);
                screenReceiver = null;
            }
        } catch (Exception ignored) {}
        super.onDestroy();
    }

    /**
     * BroadcastReceiver that handles screen ON/OFF events and counts presses.
     * Implemented as a non-static inner class so it can access the service instance.
     */
    private class ScreenReceiver extends BroadcastReceiver {
        private final Runnable resetRunnable = () -> {
            pressCount = 0;
            firstPressTime = 0;
        };

        @Override
        public void onReceive(Context context, Intent intent) {
            String action = intent.getAction();
            if (Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_OFF.equals(action) || Intent.ACTION_SCREEN_ON.equals(action)) {
                long now = System.currentTimeMillis();

                if (firstPressTime == 0 || (now - firstPressTime) > TIME_WINDOW_MS) {
                    // start a new window
                    firstPressTime = now;
                    pressCount = 1;
                } else {
                    pressCount++;
                }

                // reset counter after TIME_WINDOW_MS
                mainHandler.removeCallbacks(resetRunnable);
                mainHandler.postDelayed(resetRunnable, TIME_WINDOW_MS);

                // short debug toast so you can test behavior (remove in production)
                mainHandler.post(() -> Toast.makeText(getApplicationContext(),
                        "Power press count: " + pressCount, Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show());

                if (pressCount >= PRESS_TARGET) {
                    // Trigger SOS
                    pressCount = 0;
                    mainHandler.removeCallbacks(resetRunnable);

                    mainHandler.post(() -> {
                        Toast.makeText(getApplicationContext(), "⚠️ SOS Triggered", Toast.LENGTH_LONG).show();

                        // Launch EmergencyActivity with extra flag
                        Intent i = new Intent(getApplicationContext(), EmergencyActivity.class);
                        i.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK | Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP);
                        i.putExtra("autoHealth", true);
                        startActivity(i);
                    });
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
LoginActivity.java — student login + SharedPreferences 
package com.example.vitapemergencyalertapp;

import android.content.Intent;
import android.content.SharedPreferences;
import android.os.Bundle;
import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity;
import android.widget.Button;
import android.widget.EditText;

public class LoginActivity extends AppCompatActivity {
    EditText etName, etEmail, etBlock, etRoom;
    Button btnLogin;
    SharedPreferences prefs;

    @Override
    protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState);

        prefs = getSharedPreferences("UserPrefs", MODE_PRIVATE);

        // ✅ If data already saved, skip login
        if (prefs.contains("name") && prefs.contains("email")
                && prefs.contains("block") && prefs.contains("room")) {
            Intent i = new Intent(LoginActivity.this, EmergencyActivity.class);
            i.putExtra("name", prefs.getString("name", ""));
            i.putExtra("email", prefs.getString("email", ""));
            i.putExtra("block", prefs.getString("block", ""));
            i.putExtra("room", prefs.getString("room", ""));
            startActivity(i);
            finish(); // Prevent going back to login
            return;
        }

        setContentView(R.layout.activity_login);

        etName = findViewById(R.id.etName);
        etEmail = findViewById(R.id.etEmail);
        etBlock = findViewById(R.id.etBlock);
        etRoom = findViewById(R.id.etRoom);
        btnLogin = findViewById(R.id.btnLogin);

        // Fill saved data if partially available
        etName.setText(prefs.getString("name", ""));
        etEmail.setText(prefs.getString("email", ""));
        etBlock.setText(prefs.getString("block", ""));
        etRoom.setText(prefs.getString("room", ""));

        btnLogin.setOnClickListener(v -> {
            SharedPreferences.Editor editor = prefs.edit();
            editor.putString("name", etName.getText().toString());
            editor.putString("email", etEmail.getText().toString());
            editor.putString("block", etBlock.getText().toString());
            editor.putString("room", etRoom.getText().toString());
            editor.apply();

            Intent i = new Intent(LoginActivity.this, EmergencyActivity.class);
            i.putExtra("name", etName.getText().toString());
            i.putExtra("email", etEmail.getText().toString());
            i.putExtra("block", etBlock.getText().toString());
            i.putExtra("room", etRoom.getText().toString());
            startActivity(i);
            finish();
        });
    }
}
EmailStorage.java — persistent email address storage 
package com.example.vitapemergencyalertapp;

import android.content.Context;
import android.content.SharedPreferences;

public class EmailStorage {
    private SharedPreferences prefs;

    public EmailStorage(Context context) {
        prefs = context.getSharedPreferences("EmailPrefs", Context.MODE_PRIVATE);
    }

    public void saveEmails(String medical, String security) {
        prefs.edit().putString("medical", medical).putString("security", security).apply();
    }

    public String getMedicalEmail() {
        return prefs.getString("medical", "medical@vitap.ac.in");
    }

    public String getSecurityEmail() {
        return prefs.getString("security", "security@vitap.ac.in");
    }
}
What I Learned
  • Android SDK fundamentals: activities, intents, services, and the AccessibilityService lifecycle — all new territory from a web/Python background.
  • FusedLocationProviderClient: reliable GPS on Android, handling permission requests and fallback behaviour.
  • JavaMail SMTP: SSL vs TLS, app password setup, threading (network calls can't run on the UI thread in Android).
  • Group engineering: splitting features across 6 contributors, merging Android Studio projects, coordinating testing on different physical devices.
